summary: IDF reported multiple aerial alerts and engagements with Lebanese airspace, including two aircraft crossings and a suspected aerial target near the Lebanon border. Israeli forces killed a senior Hezbollah missile commander in the Nabatieh area and extended civilian protection times in 161 northern communities. US political statements referenced ongoing talks with Iran and a pledge of no troops to Beirut.
key_points: IDF air force intercepted two hostile launches crossing from Lebanon into Israeli territory and identified a suspicious aerial target near the Lebanon border, with no casualties reported (Source: @idf_telegram/24096).

Israeli air strike in the Nabatieh area killed Hezbollah missile commander Mohammed Mousa Mteirek, responsible for hundreds of rockets and drones targeting Israeli civilians and troops (Source: @manniefabian/58365).
Civil defense extended protection time from 60 to 90 seconds in 161 northern Israeli communities, including Haifa, the Krayot and the Carmel region (Source: @New_security8200/200867).
Hezbollah launched two rockets toward Safed in Upper Galilee and a drone struck a military position in Western Galilee; all were intercepted with no injuries (Source: @manniefabian/58371).
IDF withdrew the 146th Division from southern Lebanon after three months of operations, leaving only the 91st and 36th divisions active (Source: @manniefabian/58360).
Hezbollah commander of the missile unit was killed in an Israeli strike in southern Lebanon, according to IDF statements (Source: @lelotsenzura/92417).
Former US President Trump said talks with the Islamic Republic of Iran are proceeding rapidly and thanked observers for their attention (Source: @VahidOnline/75856).

sections.executive_summary: IDF air force intercepted two hostile launches crossing from Lebanon and identified a suspicious aerial target near the border, with no casualties (Source: @idf_telegram/24096). Israeli air strike in Nabatieh killed Hezbollah missile commander Mohammed Mousa Mteirek (Source: @manniefabian/58365). Civil defense extended protection times in 161 northern communities to 90 seconds (Source: @New_security8200/200867).

points: Former US President Trump announced that negotiations with the Islamic Republic of Iran are proceeding at a high speed and expressed gratitude for attention to the matter (Source: @VahidOnline/75856).

IDF reported two hostile aircraft launches crossing from Lebanon into Israeli territory at 01:35 and 01:46, and a suspicious aerial target was identified near the Lebanon border; no injuries were reported (Source: @idf_telegram/24096).

Israeli air force struck the Nabatieh area, killing Hezbollah missile commander Mohammed Mousa Mteirek, who oversaw hundreds of rockets and drones targeting Israeli civilians and Golani Brigade soldiers (Source: @manniefabian/58365).
Civil defense extended the protection time from 60 to 90 seconds in 161 northern localities, including Haifa, the Krayot and the Carmel, to improve civilian safety (Source: @New_security8200/200867).
Hezbollah launched two rockets toward Safed in Upper Galilee and a drone hit a military position in Western Galilee; all were intercepted with no injuries (Source: @manniefabian/58371).
Hezbollah missile commander Mohammed Mousa Mteirek was killed by an Israeli air strike in the Nabatieh area, according to IDF statements (Source: @manniefabian/58365).
IDF announced the withdrawal of the 146th Division from southern Lebanon after three months, leaving only the 91st and 36th divisions operating there (Source: @manniefabian/58360).
Hezbollah commander of the missile unit was reported killed in an Israeli strike in southern Lebanon (Source: @lelotsenzura/92417).

sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah continues to launch rockets and drones from southern Lebanon, targeting Israeli border communities, while Israeli air power remains capable of striking high-value commanders. The interception of aircraft crossing from Lebanon indicates heightened aerial vigilance and potential escalation. US political statements on Iran suggest diplomatic activity but do not directly alter the immediate threat landscape in the Israel-Lebanon frontier.
sections.regional_response: Israel has responded by extending civilian protection times and conducting targeted strikes against Hezbollah leadership, aiming to degrade missile capabilities. Hezbollah's continued attacks demonstrate resilience and intent to maintain pressure on northern Israeli towns. International actors, notably the United States, are engaged in diplomatic talks with Iran, which may influence broader regional dynamics.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Lebanese airspace should focus on low-altitude incursions and UAV activity, given recent interceptions. The loss of a senior Hezbollah missile commander may temporarily disrupt launch operations but could prompt retaliatory attacks. The withdrawal of the 146th Division suggests a scaling back of Israeli ground presence, potentially altering the tactical balance in southern Lebanon.
